title: Reopening Strait of Hormuz Would Ease Oil Crisis but Only So Much
summary: Analysts said energy and shipping companies would be reluctant to fully restore operations until they were confident that hostilities were over.

title: With Vaccines Widely Popular, Kennedy Changes Tone, but Maybe Not His Plans
summary: Several moves suggest Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. could revive his campaign to question the safety and effectiveness of the shots after the midterm elections.

title: Trump Will Participate in a Marathon Bible Reading
summary: He will read a passage from the Old Testament that his Christian supporters cite as a call to national repentance and divine blessing.

title: White House and Anthropic Hold ‘Productive’ Meeting, Aiming for a Compromise
summary: Friday’s meeting at the White House followed the introduction of Anthropic’s powerful new artificial intelligence model, Mythos, which U.S. officials believe could be critical for security.

title: Trump Expected to Loosen Restrictions on Psychedelic Drugs
summary: An upcoming executive order is intended to accelerate research into the compounds’ efficacy in treating PTSD, depression and other mental health problems, officials said.

title: The Doctor Will Seek Your Vote Now
summary: Dozens of Democratic doctors are running for office in the midterms, including some spurred by opposition to Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his anti-vaccine stance.

title: ‘Turning Point Was Charlie Kirk’: Why This Student Group Moved On
summary: Students at the University of Arkansas disagreed with Turning Point’s direction, pointing to challenges ahead for the conservative group.

title: Primary Becomes Purity Test for a State G.O.P.: ‘You Can’t Serve Two Masters’
summary: For years, Republican state legislators in Montana have been willing to team up with Democrats, but in nearly two dozen races on June 2, a nationally attuned right has those lawmakers in its sights.

title: Maduro’s Successor Is Purging Allies Who Kept Him in Power in Venezuela
summary: The successor to Venezuela’s captured President Nicolás Maduro is purging the people who kept him in power.

title: In Angola, Pope Leo XIV Faces the Legacy of Colonialism
summary: His visit includes a trip to a shrine where enslaved Africans were baptized before being forced into the treacherous voyage across the Atlantic Ocean.

title: How Trump Helped Pope Leo Find His Voice
summary: After his election last year, Leo XIV developed a reputation as a mild-mannered mediator. President Trump’s attacks have made him more combative.

title: Trump’s Dispute With Pope Leo Deepens Divisions on the Right
summary: Sean Hannity criticized the pope. Tucker Carlson attacked Mr. Hannity. And President Trump suggested ranking MAGA figures: “good, bad, and somewhere in the middle.”

title: The Pope Bedevils Trump
summary: A higher authority finally tells Trump he’s no messiah.

title: JD Vance’s Very Bad Week
summary: What recent events in Europe can tell us about the future of Trumpism.

title: Trading Spaces
summary: With spring comes the urge to spring-clean. It’s about so much more than just organizing your closet.

title: Federal Court Temporarily Freezes Nexstar’s Merger With Tegna
summary: The judge said the two television companies could not combine operations while an antitrust lawsuit proceeded. Nexstar said its deal was already done.

title: After D4vd Arrest, Lake Elsinore Residents Mourn Celeste Rivas Hernandez
summary: Celeste Rivas Hernandez went missing from Lake Elsinore, Calif., at age 13. The musician known as D4vd has been detained in connection with her death.

title: Migrant Children Removed From New York Shelter After Abuse Allegations
summary: Federal officials received complaints about the treatment of children and adolescents at a facility in Westchester County, N.Y.

title: Tornados Reported Across the Midwest as Powerful Storms Slam the Region
summary: One reported tornado downed trees and damaged cars in Lena, Ill., an official said.
